name: fred
description: FRED economic time series + ALFRED vintages: data as originally published, before revisions.
long_description: An MCP server that puts FRED® — 800,000+ U.S. and international economic time series from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis — inside your agent, including ALFRED® vintage data: any series exactly as it was known on a past date, and any single data point's full revision history from its earliest archived print to today. Search series, read observations with server-side transforms, follow the release calendar, and ask what decision-makers actually saw at the time. Read-only, live API calls with a revision-aware cache (no bulk mirroring), powered by your own free FRED API key. This product uses the FRED® API but is not endorsed or certified by the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis.

fred_api_key.title: FRED API Key
fred_api_key.description: Your own free key from https://fred.stlouisfed.org/docs/api/api_key_request.html — keys are personal under the FRED API Terms of Use.

tools.name: search_series
tools.description: Find FRED series by free-text search, best matches first.

tools.name: get_series
tools.description: One series' full metadata and verbatim notes (copyright notices included).

tools.name: get_observations
tools.description: A date window of values with optional transform and downsampling.

tools.name: get_latest
tools.description: The most recent value plus the next scheduled release date.

tools.name: compare_series
tools.description: Align 2-5 series on one date axis for comparison.

tools.name: get_release_calendar
tools.description: Upcoming data-release dates ("when is the next jobs report?").

tools.name: get_series_as_of
tools.description: A series exactly as it was known on a past date (ALFRED vintage).

tools.name: get_revision_history
tools.description: One data point's life across revisions: earliest archived print to today.

tools.name: get_vintage_dates
tools.description: When a series' data was released or revised (its ALFRED vintages).
